Spokane has united in an invita-
ton being sent by the cities of the
west to Theodore Roosevelt to re
turn from his African hunting tour
via the Pacific. On behalf of this
city the invitation was forwarded
to the Denver Post, which has the
matter in hand, by Mayor Pratt
and Secretary Monroe of the cham
Der of commerce.


Action on the ordinance creating
a city purchasing agent was de
ferred two weeks by the city coun
Gil last night, on the request of the
board of public works.

able contr buttons for 1910: Chill
drens home, 5000; st. Joseph's Or.
phanage, 5000; Salvation Army
Rescue home, $500; Florence Crit
tenton home, $500; House of the
Good Shepherd, $500.

gress will be resumed on the ar
rival here next week from Denver

secretary. The congress will be
held the week of the Interstate fair

the Farmers State bank of Me-
Perez, Idaho, said that the whea

as in the Palouse. Lands sell from
